task: Write a Python function nth_smallest(nums, n) returning the nth smallest distinct value in the list, counting from 1. Return None when n is less than 1 or exceeds the number of distinct values. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The code correctly handles edge cases, including an edge case where n exceeds the number of distinct values.
trace: I approached this task by first making sure the list only contains unique values and sorting it in ascending order. To handle cases where the number of distinct values is less than n or n is less than or equal to 0, I returned None. Otherwise, I returned the requested nth smallest value from the sorted list. The edge case I handled was when there are fewer distinct numbers than the specified 'n'.
